title: Illegal medical devices to feed newborns were sold around world

input.lead: Medicina Ltd and Kenneth Harrison, from Bolton, are convicted of several historical fraud offences
input.summary: Medicina Ltd and Kenneth Harrison, from Bolton, are convicted of several historical fraud offences
input.evidence_text: A company and its director have been found guilty of illegally supplying specialist medical equipment for feeding newborn babies in hospital critical care settings around the world Medicina Ltd and Kenneth Harrison, from Bolton, were convicted of several fraud offences relating to feeding pumps and tubes for babies as well as blunt fill needle devices used to draw up and prepare fluids and medications The company managed to get its devices into hospitals in the UK, Spain, Australia and New Zealand The successful prosecution came after a complex 13-year investigation, although Medicina has said the offences took place under different management The fraud unravelled after the Medicines and Healthcare Products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) first identified concerns in 2013 Its investigation found a number of devices, including enteral feeding pumps and gastrostomy "button" devices used to provide feed directly into the stomach of very sick newborn babies, were supplied with CE markings that were not valid

title: Man wants to inspire others to be 'proud of their prosthetics'

input.lead: Michael Hopkinson was among the models taking part in a fashion show for an adaptive clothing brand
input.summary: Michael Hopkinson was among the models taking part in a fashion show for an adaptive clothing brand
input.evidence_text: A man who had both his legs amputated in 2024 after getting an infection has become a catwalk model in a bid to inspire others Michael Hopkinson, from Manchester, was among the models who were showcasing the latest line from Recondition - a brand set up to create adaptive clothing The 39-year-old said he wanted other people to be proud of their prostheses "People have a lot of shame about prosthetics and not showing them and even if just one person who is going through amputations sees this and goes actually... I'm going through this and I can relate," he said Hopkinson added: "That's how I think it works - it's all about relatability, and this is a very good opportunity to do that." Models travelled from across the country to take part in the fashion show at the Aviva Studios in Manchester

title: Tree removal objectors work with council on Poynton Pool safety plan

input.lead: Campaigners and a council who rowed over trees being chopped down are now working together
input.summary: Campaigners and a council who rowed over trees being chopped down are now working together
input.evidence_text: Tree removal objectors work with council on Poynton Pool safety plan - BBC News Image caption, Poynton Pool is a popular local beauty spot By Kaleigh Watterson Cheshire political reporter Published 2 hours ago Campaigners, who have been protesting for years about trees being chopped down at a beauty spot, are now working with the council on how to carry out the safety works needed. A new proposal, drawn up by the Friends of Poynton Pool and Cheshire East Council, follows the current footpath and is designed to "avoid as many trees as possible". The campaign group and the authority said they had "agreed a preferred way forward" and the new proposals included "relatively minor engineering works". Cheshire East Council first outlined plans to remove trees at the ornament pond, which dates back to 1750, in 2023, sparking demonstrations outside Macclesfield Town Hall. Because of its size, Poynton Pool is legally classed as a reservoir. As a result, the council said it was required to do safety works on the site. Image caption, Campaigners protested outside council meetings, including this one in Macclesfield in January 2024 The tree removal plan led to protests both at Poynton Pool and Cheshire East Council meetings in recent years. In January, Cheshire East Council's strategic planning board refused planning permission for the scheme . At that meeting, planning officers said they had received a letter from the housing secretary which said they could not grant permission without his permission. But officers said thst did not stop councillors voting on the plan, and the application was refused with five councillors voting against the plans, and four in favour. Months later, Cheshire East Council, the Friends of Poynton Pool and Poynton Town Council, have issued a joint statem

title: Post-England match breath tests planned in Greater Manchester

input.lead: Greater Manchester Police says it will be carrying out roadside breath tests after England's match
input.summary: Greater Manchester Police says it will be carrying out roadside breath tests after England's match
input.evidence_text: Police have warned people watching England's World Cup clash with Mexico that it will be carrying out roadside drink-driving tests on Monday The game was due to kick off at 01:00 BST in Mexico City, and some pubs and bars in the region were expected to stay open late to allow fans to watch Thomas Tuchel's men attempt to win a place in the quarter-finals A spokesman for Greater Manchester Police said: "With the match taking place later in the evening, some people may continue drinking into the early hours "While you may feel fit to drive the following morning, alcohol can remain in your system for longer than you think and you could still be over the legal limit." On Friday, the National Police Chiefs Council criticised the timing of the government's decision to let pubs stay open until 05:00 BST on Monday It said the "late announcement" meant officers would have to be taken away from other duties and work longer hours, even though the team's likely route through the tournament "has been known for a considerable time"

title: Two arrested in Bolton for obstructing police helping crash victim

input.lead: Two men have been arrested for obstructing police officers following the incident, GMP says
input.summary: Two men have been arrested for obstructing police officers following the incident, GMP says
input.evidence_text: Two arrested in Bolton for obstructing police helping crash victim - BBC News Image source, PA Media Image caption, Two men have been arrested for obstructing police officers following the incident, a force spokesperson said By Angela Ferguson North West Published 5 July 2026 An 18-year-old man is in a critical condition in hospital after reports that his off-road bike struck a stationary car, police said. Officers then arrested two men on suspicion of obstructing them from helping the man, who had crashed on St Helens Road, Bolton, at about 22:18 BST on Saturday. A force spokesperson the man was given first aid before he was taken to hospital with serious injuries, where he remains in a critical condition. The two men were arrested on suspicion of obstructing police and a Section 5 Public Order Act offence. title: When Pele's Brazil team lived in a Cheshire hotel

input.lead: Laurence Bennett was able to meet the Brazil national side during their stay in Lymm
input.summary: Laurence Bennett was able to meet the Brazil national side during their stay in Lymm
input.evidence_text: A man has described the "amazing" moment Pele and his Brazil teammates visited his hometown in Cheshire The South American side stayed at a hotel in Lymm during their 1966 World Cup campaign, which saw them dumped out of the competition at the group stage But as part of their time in the rural village, the squad, which also included Garrincha and Jairzinho, met with locals and signed autographs Sixty years on, Laurence Bennett, who was just five years old at the time, said he would spend mornings before school serving toast to the players as his mum Bessie worked at the Lymm Hotel "I got to just wander around these magnificent footballers," Bennett told BBC Radio Manchester He said the access people in the village had to the players would never be allowed today

title: Why voting method change matters in mayoral poll

input.lead: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.summary: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.evidence_text: The Labour Party could benefit most from the return of the Supplementary Votes (SV) system in the Greater Manchester mayoral race, a polling expert has said Voters in the region head to the polls on 30 July and will be able to select a first and second choice of candidate as SV comes into replace the first-past-the-post (FPTP) system used at the last election A number of candidates have already declared for the contest , triggered after Andy Burnham's successful campaign to become the MP of Makerfield, leaving the post he has held since 2017 vacant Prof Jonathan Tonge of the University of Liverpool said Labour could pick up second preference votes from Greens and Lib Dems, while Reform could be less likely to see Restore voters put them as second preference due to "bad blood" between the party leaders The system was used in the first two mayoral elections, but was scrapped by the last Conservative government in favour of FPTP system, before it was reintroduced again by Labour Under SCV, candidates need to win more than 50% of the vote in the first round to be come mayor or the top two candidates go into a run-off where second preference votes from eliminated candidates are counted

title: What is the Greater Manchester mayor and what do they do?

input.lead: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.summary: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

title: Greater Manchester's mayoral election explained

input.lead: Voters in all 10 boroughs will elect a new mayor to replace Andy Burnham on 30 July
input.summary: Voters in all 10 boroughs will elect a new mayor to replace Andy Burnham on 30 July
input.evidence_text: People living in Greater Manchester will head to the polls in July to vote for who they think should be the region's mayor Residents living in all 10 boroughs will vote in the Greater Manchester mayoral by-election on 30 July We take a look at why it is happening, what the timetable is, and how the supplementary vote system works The election was triggered when Andy Burnham, who held the office for nine years, was elected the MP for Makerfield in last Thursday's parliamentary by-election By law, an MP cannot sit in the House of Commons and hold a metropolitan mayor role that has powers over local policing The Greater Manchester mayoral contest has to take place within 35 days of the post becoming vacant

title: How do I register to vote in the May 2026 elections?

input.lead: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.summary: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.evidence_text: Click to expand How do I register to vote? Millions of voters across England, Scotland and Wales are heading to the polls on 7 May. Depending where you are, you might be voting to elect your local council members, a new mayor, members of the Welsh Senedd, or Scottish Parliament But if you're going to vote, you need to make sure you are registered. The deadline for registering to vote has passed but there's still time to apply for a postal or proxy vote Here's what you need to know about how and when you register to vote Registering only takes a few minutes if you do it online. It’s the same process whether you plan to vote in person at a polling station, by post, or through a proxy voter Go to the gov.uk website and fill out the form. You'll need your National Insurance number and current address details to hand If you can't provide your NI number you can still complete the online form but you'll need to provide documents like a passport or utility bill to your local electoral registration office before you're properly registered

title: Thousands take part in Manchester Italian Madonna del Rosario march

input.lead: The procession features a life-sized statue of the Virgin Mary, dating back to the 1880s
input.summary: The procession features a life-sized statue of the Virgin Mary, dating back to the 1880s
input.evidence_text: More than 2,000 people will parade through Manchester later in a tradition begun by the city's Italian community nearly 140 years ago The Madonna Del Rosario procession is part of a display of religious faith but also a celebration of Italian identity with pride of place given to a 5ft (1.5m) statue of the Virgin Mary dating back nearly 100 years It begins at 13:00 BST in George Leigh Street, Ancoats, an area once known as Little Italy due to the Italian immigrants who settled there The procession, which began in 1888, leaves Ancoats with association officials and civic dignitaries at its head, led by the Lord Mayor of Manchester and the Bishop of Salford The procession involves the carrying of religious emblems – The Madonna, The Calvary, St Anthony and banners – from Ancoats across Manchester city centre, passing the Town Hall into St Peter's Square around 14:30 After a pause it returns back to George Leigh Street to finish with a short blessing outside the former St Michael's Church

title: Bury illustrator's artwork signed by Prince William

input.lead: Myroslava Coates drew a timeline showcasing Prince William's homelessness charity's achievements
input.summary: Myroslava Coates drew a timeline showcasing Prince William's homelessness charity's achievements
input.evidence_text: An illustrator known for her murals and window art has had one of her creations signed by The Prince of Wales Myroslava Coates, from Prestwich, Bury, was asked to draw a timeline showcasing the achievements of Prince William's homelessness prevention programme, Homewards The artwork was on display at an event at the Tate Modern marking the charity's three‑year anniversary Coates said meeting the future king there and having him sign her work was "massive" Coates added: "It felt like meeting a friend. I wasn't expecting to have a chat with him and then when they asked if I was comfortable, I was like, 'Oh my gosh, yeah'." The artist told BBC Radio Manchester her timeline illustration started in 1993, when Princess Dianna took William to The Passage homelessness charity in London

title: Donna Keogh: Second murder arrest over 1998 disappearance

input.lead: A 62-year-old man is questioned on suspicion of murdering Donna Keogh who was last seen in 1998
input.summary: A 62-year-old man is questioned on suspicion of murdering Donna Keogh who was last seen in 1998
input.evidence_text: A second man has been arrested on suspicion of murdering a teenager who went missing 28 years ago Donna Keogh, 17, was last seen in Middlesbrough on 28 April 1998 and is known to have travelled to Leeds during that year. Detectives said it was "strongly suspected" she was murdered shortly after she disappeared A 62-year-old man was arrested in the Manchester area and would be interviewed in police custody, Cleveland Police said Earlier this year, officers arrested a 64-year-old man from the Leeds area, also on suspicion of murder. He remained on bail while the investigation continued, police said Her body has never been found despite a decades-long investigation Deputy senior investigating officer, Det Insp Evan Kirtley, said her family had lived in "unbearable uncertainty" for nearly 30 years

Depending where you are, you might be voting to elect your local council members, a new mayor, members of the Welsh Senedd, or Scottish Parliament But if you're going to vote, you need to make sure you are registered. The deadline for registering to vote has passed but there's still time to apply for a postal or proxy vote Here's what you need to know about how and when you register to vote Registering only takes a few minutes if you do it online. It’s the same process whether you plan to vote in person at a polling station, by post, or through a proxy voter Go to the gov.uk website and fill out the form. You'll need your National Insurance number and current address details to hand If you can't provide your NI number you can still complete the online form but you'll need to provide documents like a passport or utility bill to your local electoral registration office before you're properly registered

title: Thousands take part in Manchester Italian Madonna del Rosario march

input.evidence_text: More than 2,000 people will parade through Manchester later in a tradition begun by the city's Italian community nearly 140 years ago The Madonna Del Rosario procession is part of a display of religious faith but also a celebration of Italian identity with pride of place given to a 5ft (1.5m) statue of the Virgin Mary dating back nearly 100 years It begins at 13:00 BST in George Leigh Street, Ancoats, an area once known as Little Italy due to the Italian immigrants who settled there The procession, which began in 1888, leaves Ancoats with association officials and civic dignitaries at its head, led by the Lord Mayor of Manchester and the Bishop of Salford The procession involves the carrying of religious emblems – The Madonna, The Calvary, St Anthony and banners – from Ancoats across Manchester city centre, passing the Town Hall into St Peter's Square around 14:30 After a pause it returns back to George Leigh Street to finish with a short blessing outside the former St Michael's Church

input.evidence_text: A second man has been arrested on suspicion of murdering a teenager who went missing 28 years ago Donna Keogh, 17, was last seen in Middlesbrough on 28 April 1998 and is known to have travelled to Leeds during that year. Detectives said it was "strongly suspected" she was murdered shortly after she disappeared A 62-year-old man was arrested in the Manchester area and would be interviewed in police custody, Cleveland Police said Earlier this year, officers arrested a 64-year-old man from the Leeds area, also on suspicion of murder. He remained on bail while the investigation continued, police said Her body has never been found despite a decades-long investigation Deputy senior investigating officer, Det Insp Evan Kirtley, said her family had lived in "unbearable uncertainty" for nearly 30 years

input.evidence_text: Mental health An army career turned his life around - then it unravelled Gary Hind's anxiety led to physical pain that saw him physically floored News Stephen Topping Senior reporter 19:16, 05 Jul 2026 View Image Army veteran Gary Hind saw his life sp An army veteran whose military career 'turned his life around' was left physically floored as his anxiety spiralled. Gary Hind, from Hyde , lived with mental health difficulties from childhood following a troubled home situation and the sudden death of his father But the 66-year-old managed to get his life firmly on track with a career in the army. Gary joined the armed forces aged 16, and he credits the military for shaping the man he became In 1999, he received an MBE in recognition of his service. Gary went on to enjoy a new career, until events took a turn for the worse - leading to a mental health spiral that saw him suffer tightness in his chest, heart flutters and breathlessness After a difficult start in life, Gary says the army 'turned his life around'. He said: “Being in the army rebuilt me as a person. I thrived and learnt a lot of discipline and control. I felt like a new person.” When his military career came to an end, Gary found a job he loved helping students with their personal development skills and extra-curricular activities. But after 15 years in the role, a difficult period at work left him feeling unsettled and unsupported

It was announced last week that pubs would be able to stay open until 5am to host and stream the match to punters But in a new warning issued before the match, Greater Manchester Police said that officers would be conducting stops of vehicles across the region to check for any potential drug or drink driving offences after a night watching the match Due to the match taking place at the Azteca Stadium in Mexico City, the match has a later start time than other matches have done and it meant that its kick-off did not fall into the originally allowed rules of when pubs could extend their opening hours until And despite rumours FIFA might bring the kick off forward to 7pm on Sunday night, it was later confirmed kick off would remain at 1am on Monday morning - which was later put back to 2am due to thunderstorms

Questions are being asked over delays in the building of more than 270 homes in the Salford district of Ordsall in a scheme being managed by the council's development company Dérive The plans, which include 100 much-needed affordable houses in what is still one of the city's most deprived areas, were first approved in March 2024. It was hoped the 'vibrant, inclusive' estate's first residents would have moved in by now But more than two years on not a single brick has been laid. The two plots off Robert Hall Street were once home to a school and a shopping precinct But they have been fenced off and left to stand empty ever since they cleared of trees and groundworks carried out in the summer of 2024. And in the meantime residents say the site is becoming more and more of an eyesore

input.evidence_text: Manchester weather Met Office latest on when another heatwave will hit Greater Manchester It's going to be hot once again News Stephen Topping Senior reporter 15:23, 05 Jul 2026 View Image Another heatwave is on the way (Image: Met Office) If you're glad to be cooling down after the last heatwave , brace yourself for what lies ahead this week. Another heatwave is hitting the UK - and it is due in Greater Manchester before next weekend The Met Office is forecasting plenty of sunshine across the region this week. Temperatures will be more mild at the beginning of the week, before ramping up as the days go on Highs of 22C are currently forecast on Monday (July 6) and Tuesday. An overcast start to Monday should make way for sunny intervals from late morning, continuing throughout the rest of the day, with Tuesday following a similar pattern While some parts of the country are already experiencing another heatwave at the start of the week, Greater Manchester is due to warm up from Wednesday. Highs of 27C are forecast for Wednesday, with 29C on both Thursday and Friday, and 28C on Saturday - with clear skies forecast Overnight temperatures will be above average throughout the week, although they are thankfully not expected to reach 20C like in the June heatwave. Lows of between 14C and 16C are forecast over the next three nights, while they could reach 18C later in the week

The representative for Tyldesley and Mosley won his seat with almost 45 per cent Now campaign group Hope Not Hate claim to have uncovered several posts between 2019 and 2025 that they’ve described as ‘awful and bizarre’. At the time of writing, the posts are publicly visible on a personal profile on Facebook, where coun White has also shared his councillor de One 2018 post on the account features an image of former Conservative cabinet member and House of Lords member Michael Heseltine, with the caption: “Lord Haw Haw… Shoot him and his fellow parasites! Fill yer pockets… Dirty money!” A year later, when calls were being made for then Prime Minister Theresa May to resign, another post reads: “Champagne back on ice again… can’t we just shoot her… please.”

It was announced last week that pubs would be able to stay open until 5am to host and stream the match to punters But in a new warning issued before the match, Greater Manchester Police said that officers would be conducting stops of vehicles across the region to check for any potential drug or drink driving offences after a night watching the match Due to the match taking place at the Azteca Stadium in Mexico City, the match has a later start time than other matches have done and it meant that its kick-off did not fall into the originally allowed rules of when pubs could extend their opening hours until And despite rumours FIFA might bring the kick off forward to 7pm on Sunday night, it was later confirmed kick off would remain at 1am on Monday morning - which was later put back to 2am due to thunderstorms

input.evidence_text: Mental health An army career turned his life around - then it unravelled Gary Hind's anxiety led to physical pain that saw him physically floored News Stephen Topping Senior reporter 19:16, 05 Jul 2026 View Image Army veteran Gary Hind saw his life sp An army veteran whose military career 'turned his life around' was left physically floored as his anxiety spiralled. Gary Hind, from Hyde , lived with mental health difficulties from childhood following a troubled home situation and the sudden death of his father But the 66-year-old managed to get his life firmly on track with a career in the army. Gary joined the armed forces aged 16, and he credits the military for shaping the man he became In 1999, he received an MBE in recognition of his service. Gary went on to enjoy a new career, until events took a turn for the worse - leading to a mental health spiral that saw him suffer tightness in his chest, heart flutters and breathlessness After a difficult start in life, Gary says the army 'turned his life around'. He said: “Being in the army rebuilt me as a person. I thrived and learnt a lot of discipline and control. I felt like a new person.” When his military career came to an end, Gary found a job he loved helping students with their personal development skills and extra-curricular activities. But after 15 years in the role, a difficult period at work left him feeling unsettled and unsupported

input.evidence_text: Salford Hundreds of people were meant to be living here by now - instead it looks like a 'bad day in Beirut' 'It's been abandoned and we've been abandoned' News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 18:42, 05 Jul 2026 View 2 Images The empty plot of land on Robert By now this desolate plot of wasteland should have been home to hundreds of people living on tree-lined streets on a brand new eco-friendly estate Instead it looks, in the words of one local resident, like a 'bad day in Beirut'. Questions are being asked over delays in the building of more than 270 homes in the Salford district of Ordsall in a scheme being managed by the council's development company Dérive The plans, which include 100 much-needed affordable houses in what is still one of the city's most deprived areas, were first approved in March 2024. It was hoped the 'vibrant, inclusive' estate's first residents would have moved in by now But more than two years on not a single brick has been laid. The two plots off Robert Hall Street were once home to a school and a shopping precinct But they have been fenced off and left to stand empty ever since they cleared of trees and groundworks carried out in the summer of 2024. A clip, shared online, appeared to show the white dog trying to wriggle out of the collar with claims made that it was 'kept chained up at a scrapyard' A post by dog trainer and animal welfare campaigner Sammy Wilkinson received hundreds of responses and comments after it was shared on Thursday (July 2). It was claimed that the incident had been reported to the RSPCA However, a further update on social media then claimed that the owners had 'agreed to let the dog go if we can find a suitable home for him.' In a response, the RSPCA said it was 'aware of the situation' but could not comment on individual complaints or what action may or may not have been taken. input.evidence_text: Mental health An army career turned his life around - then it unravelled Gary Hind's anxiety led to physical pain that saw him physically floored News Stephen Topping Senior reporter 19:16, 05 Jul 2026 View Image Army veteran Gary Hind saw his life sp An army veteran whose military career 'turned his life around' was left physically floored as his anxiety spiralled. Gary Hind, from Hyde , lived with mental health difficulties from childhood following a troubled home situation and the sudden death of his father But the 66-year-old managed to get his life firmly on track with a career in the army. Gary joined the armed forces aged 16, and he credits the military for shaping the man he became In 1999, he received an MBE in recognition of his service. Gary went on to enjoy a new career, until events took a turn for the worse - leading to a mental health spiral that saw him suffer tightness in his chest, heart flutters and breathlessness After a difficult start in life, Gary says the army 'turned his life around'. He said: “Being in the army rebuilt me as a person. I thrived and learnt a lot of discipline and control. I felt like a new person.” When his military career came to an end, Gary found a job he loved helping students with their personal development skills and extra-curricular activities. But after 15 years in the role, a difficult period at work left him feeling unsettled and unsupported

Questions are being asked over delays in the building of more than 270 homes in the Salford district of Ordsall in a scheme being managed by the council's development company Dérive The plans, which include 100 much-needed affordable houses in what is still one of the city's most deprived areas, were first approved in March 2024. It was hoped the 'vibrant, inclusive' estate's first residents would have moved in by now But more than two years on not a single brick has been laid. The two plots off Robert Hall Street were once home to a school and a shopping precinct But they have been fenced off and left to stand empty ever since they cleared of trees and groundworks carried out in the summer of 2024. input.evidence_text: Stockport The derelict Stockport mill that has seen 'illegal raves' but could now get new lease of life Compstall Mill helped build the area around it but today is only home to pigeons. However there is new hope the site could be brought back to life Nestled away in a quiet Stockport village, an abandoned mill could be given a new lease of life. Compstall Mill helped build the area around it and once housed cotton mill workers but today is only home to pigeons The building was built in the 1830s with the nearby village of Compstall built by businessman George Andrew to house his 800 workers at the mills. The village’s waterways were also built by Andrew to supply water from the weir to turn massive wheels supplying the mill’s power and After its days as a cotton factory, the site became home to a number of businesses including a gym though these have all now moved out. On a tour of the site, the Manchester Evening News saw how the roofs of various buildings have now collapsed and the damage left by a devastatin Despite the multiple warning signs and clearly unsafe buildings, the Manchester Evening News was told the mill was attracting urban explorers and teenagers trying to see where they can find while illegal raves took place at the back of the site during the COVID pandemic with ‘1,0 View 5 Images The Compstall Mill plans would look to transform the current mill and radically improve it as well as the area around it (Image: Buju Architects/Compstall Mill 2025 Limited)

input.evidence_text: Manchester Nine pieces of Greater Manchester history at risk of being lost forever Pubs, an old bank, and an elegant shopping mall are on a national at risk list Comments News Neal Keeling Chief reporter 07:32, 05 Jul 2026 View 13 Images A once much-l There are nine new Greater Manchester entries on the SAVE Britain's Heritage Buildings At Risk Register. Also identified as in danger of being lost forever are a Georgian canal warehouse; a "striking" red-bricked factory in Trafford ; a former bank; Victorian solicitors offices; One of them is Barton Arcade, which sits between Deansgate and St Ann's Square in Manchester city centre. It was completed in 1871 by the firm Corbett, Raby & Sawyer and was inspired by iconic European destinations such as Milan’s Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II and Sir Joseph Paxt It stretches 226 feet wide and 53 feet tall under striking glass and iron roof and is named for the developer, John Hope Barton Among its earliest and most celebrated tenants was Stephenson’s Glass & Chinaware, a retailer known for elegant ceramics and fine household goods, which remained a fixture of the arcade for nearly 100 years, until 1967 But the arcade was not exclusively for shops. In the late 1870s, it was home to the Humane Society for the Hundred of Salford , which provided emergency rescue equipment for canal workers. In the 1880s, a phrenologist, practising the art of reading skull shapes, set up shop here

The massive billboard, which displays a changing roll of adverts, will now have to removed by Aug It came after Manchester council issued a 'discontinuation notice' on the land ordering the hoarding to be taken down. The council said the 'dominating' hoarding was 'contrary to the overall aims of the regeneration strategies for New Cross, NOMA and the Northern gateway' Because the illuminated sign required 'increased cognitive demand' it also had the potential to cause a 'severe' distraction for drivers and be 'detrimental' to safety, a council inspector said. The Appeal Court heard planning permission for the hoarding was first granted in Sept In 2021 it was replaced by the current digital billboard. A council inspector who visited the site in 2024 described the 'very large' billboard as 'intrusive' and 'overbearing'

input.evidence_text: Courts The horrific past of murderer who kidnapped girl, 15, and abused her Former radio presenter Paul Sharpe is awaiting his fate from the courts once again News Stephen Topping Senior reporter 19:43, 04 Jul 2026 View 2 Images Paul Sharpe, 56, from A kidnapper who tied a girl up in his car and abused her previously killed a mum who he struck up a relationship with, the Manchester Evening News can reveal. Paul Sharpe is awaiting his fate after abducting a 15-year-old girl at knifepoint, forcing her into his car and sexually The 56-year-old, from Stockport , has admitted his latest sickening crimes and will be sentenced later this year. But it is not the first the Sharpe has committed an abhorrent crime against a female in Greater Manchester Back in October 2000, Sharpe murdered a Bolton woman who he was in a relationship with. The pair had struck up a romance after she listened to Sharpe on a radio show he hosted at the time on Tower FM, under his alias Paul Shaw Sharpe fatally strangled the 28-year-old mum, a beauty therapy student at Bolton College, after she threatened to break up with him. He went on the run, but three days later handed himself in, telling officers he had 'flipped' after the relationship appeared to be reaching an end Sharpe, then of Wellington Road, Bury, was handed a life sentence at Manchester Crown Court the following March. The judge, Mr Justice Penry-Davey, told him: "You deprived her of her life and her son of his mother."

title: The Greater Manchester council warned it could soon be breaking the law

input.lead: Oldham The Greater Manchester council warned it could soon be breaking the law Elected members have been told they have two weeks to come up with someone to lead them or things could get much worse.
input.summary: Oldham The Greater Manchester council warned it could soon be breaking the law Elected members have been told they have two weeks to come up with someone to lead them or things could get much worse. Comments News Edward Barnes Local Democracy Reporter Oldham councillors have been
input.evidence_text: Oldham The Greater Manchester council warned it could soon be breaking the law Elected members have been told they have two weeks to come up with someone to lead them or things could get much worse. Comments News Edward Barnes Local Democracy Reporter Oldham councillors have been warned the local authority could soon be breaking the law, as a top boss issues a warning. Elected members have been told they have two weeks to come up with someone to lead them or things could get much worse After no one was elected as Oldham Council’s leader on July 1, the local authority is still in limbo with officers continuing to use emergency powers just to keep services running . Key political decisions cannot be made and multiple meetings have been cancelled as a result The recent local elections on May 5 left no party with a majority or even a third of seats in the council chamber. Bids to lead the council put forward by Labour , Reform UK, and an alliance of different parties have all been rejected with everyone blaming each other for the dead Some progress has been made but the council is not able to function normally without a political leadership to drive the organisation forward. One councillor suggested to the Manchester Evening News the situation inside the council is now akin to ‘a poisoned lake’ View 2 Images Councillors meeting on July 1 (Image: Copyright Unknown)

title: Andy Burnham wins Makerfield by-election: What happens next with Greater Manchester mayoralty?

input.lead: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade.
input.summary: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A
input.evidence_text: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK law strictly dictates that the office holder cannot simultaneously serve as an MP

title: Major disruption on Metrolink services this month as track replaced between Trafford Bar and Deansgate - About Manchester

input.lead: Specialist lifting equipment will be used in the UK for the first time when upgrades to Metrolink continue this month, with tracks being replaced at Trafford Bar and Deansgate-Castlefield.
input.summary: Specialist lifting equipment will be used in the UK for the first time when upgrades to Metrolink continue this month, with tracks being replaced at Trafford Bar and Deansgate-Castlefield. Transport for Greater Manchester (TfGM) will also replace several lifts at tram stations at
input.evidence_text: Specialist lifting equipment will be used in the UK for the first time when upgrades to Metrolink continue this month, with tracks being replaced at Trafford Bar and Deansgate-Castlefield. Transport for Greater Manchester (TfGM) will also replace several lifts at tram stations at From Monday 13 July to Sunday 2 August, TfGM will replace around 1.6km of track – parts of which are more than 30 years old – at Trafford Bar and Deansgate-Castlefield, as well as working on a separate section on the Eccles line at the same time The track between Trafford Bar and St Peter’s Square is one of the busiest sections of light-rail in the world, with one tram passing through every 30 seconds for 20 hours a day, seven days a week While the work is being done, no trams will run on the Eccles, Trafford Centre and Altrincham lines. Services on the East Didsbury and Manchester Airport lines will terminate at Firswood, while services on the Rochdale line will terminate at Exchange Square Replacement buses will run between affected stops, with normal services due to resume from Monday 3 August. There will be a special event bus service running for the cricket at Old Trafford on 26 and 31 July (The Hundred, Manchester Super Giants vs Birmingham and then Trent Rocke Work will also take place at Eccles and the city centre around Piccadilly Gardens during this closure (13 July-29 July). This will help to reduce the need for further disruptions to the network

Over the following hours he moved between different car parks, forcing her to drink alcohol and sexually assaulting her The girl’s mother reported her missing, prompting a major police investigation. title: Historic rail tour to whisk Altrincham passengers to Shakespeare's Stratford

input.lead: The special charter train will set off on Saturday 22nd August
input.summary: The special charter train will set off on Saturday 22nd August
input.evidence_text: The special charter train will set off on Saturday 22nd August Share on WhatsApp Share via Email Rail travellers along the Mid Cheshire Line are being invited to enjoy a unique day out in August aboard The Retro Cestrian Bard, a special charter train bound for Stratford-upon-Avon Promoted by the Mid Cheshire Rail Users' Association (MCRUA) and operated by Retro Railtours, the excursion on Saturday 22nd August will pick up passengers at Altrincham, Knutsford, Northwich and Chester before heading south to the famous birthplace of William Shakespeare The return leg will take a different, more scenic route, threading through the Malvern Hills and along the Welsh Marches, giving passengers a chance to take in some of Britain's finest railway landscapes The train itself promises to be as much a part of the day out as the destination. Comfortable Mk3 air-conditioned coaches will be hauled by Class 37 diesel locomotives, with every passenger guaranteed a reserved seat Once in Stratford-upon-Avon, passengers will have around three hours to explore the town's historic streets, riverside walks, theatres and the many attractions linked to Shakespeare's life and work

title: New outdoor basketball court opens in Cringle Park as part of city-wide investment in community sport

input.evidence_text: Manchester City Council, in partnership with Manchester Basketball and the National Governing Body Basketball England, has completed a major refurbishment of the outdoor basketball court at Cringle Park in Levenshulme The project has seen the existing court fully transformed, including a deep clean, a brand-new playing surface, updated posts and hoops, and a vibrant new colour design to create a high-quality and welcoming space for local residents The investment is part of a wider programme to improve community sport facilities across Manchester. Work is also scheduled to take place at Eggington Street Park in Harpurhey, where two courts will be overhauled ahead of the summer The programme is supported by a £47,000 investment from Manchester City Council and Manchester Basketball, alongside a contribution from Basketball England The new courts will be supported by a programme of community activity and activation sessions, including opportunities for local young people to engage with players from Manchester Basketball The city is also home to Basketball England and hosts a wide range of programmes, from junior leagues to inclusive sessions for disabled and SEN participants

title: Repurposed flats will help homeless Manchester families

input.lead: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families
input.summary: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families
input.evidence_text: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families The property in Greenwood Road, Woodhouse Park, has been acquired by the Council from registered housing provider Mosscare St Vincent’s as part of wider plans to boost the city’s stock of publicly-owned, quality temporary accommodation Work is due to start this summer, and be completed in December this year, to refurbish the property as 17 self-contained flats for homeless families from Wythenshawe and south Manchester All flats will be refurbished, including new kitchens and bathrooms in each case. Two new laundry rooms for shared use will also be created, and gardens to the rear will have raised bedding for residents’ use The new accommodation will see families supported by an accommodation team based on site to help them move on as quickly as possible into permanent settled tenancies The Greenwood Road initiative is the latest example of the Council’s drive to increase its temporary accommodation stock across the city to reduce the number of out-of-area placements, helping people who become homeless to stay closer to their previous homes and support networks

input.evidence_text: Oldham Council is launching the new Oldham Advice Service from today (Wednesday 1 July) Oldham Advice Service is a free, confidential and impartial advice service delivered on behalf of Oldham Council by Citizens Advice It offers support on money, debt, housing, benefits and financial wellbeing and much more all in one place The service is funded by Oldham Council through the UK Government’s Crisis and Resilience Fund The need to provide timely support has never been more urgent. From the rising cost of energy to food and housing, we want to make sure that residents know where to go to get friendly and helpful support whatever they are going through Nationally, almost half of those who come to Citizens Advice about debt are already at the point of crisis when they first make contact. By that stage, options are narrower and the personal toll, on mental health, family life and finances are already significant

title: More than a game: football's role in one family's fostering journey - Rochdale Borough Council

input.lead: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care
input.summary: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care
input.evidence_text: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care Lauren (46) and her husband Danny Sullivan (52) have fostered for Rochdale Borough Council since 2012 and currently care for 5 young lads aged 12 to 21, alongside their 9-year-old son, Daniel Their home is very busy as you can imagine and ‘the washing machine is non-stop’ Lauren jokes. Each child has their own interests, including things like the gym and gaming, but football is what stands out as the cement in their daily routines From training and coaching on weekdays to refereeing and playing matches at the weekend, the house is football mad and it’s their passion for the sport which connects them as a family and what has helped the lads to create a strong brotherly bond “They’ll all come home after school, college or work, and Daniel will ask them for a game,” Lauren said, with a pair of football nets visible outside from the living room “It’s so lovely to see the smiles on their faces when they’re all playing together in the back garden.”

title: New funding will help the council tackle the sticky issue of chewing gum on Rochdale's streets - Rochdale Borough Council

input.evidence_text: Rochdale Borough Council has been awarded a grant from the Chewing Gum Task Force, administered by environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy, to help clean up gum and reduce gum littering Today, the council announced plans to step up the removal of chewing gum that blights Rochdale town centre after receiving a £27,461 grant to tackle the issue Rochdale is one of 50 councils across the country that have successfully applied to the Chewing Gum Task Force, now in its fifth year, for funds to clean gum off pavements and prevent it from being littered in the first place The Chewing Gum Task Force grant scheme – established by Defra (Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs) and run by environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy – is open to councils across the UK who wish to clean up gum in their local areas and invest in long-term behavio The Task Force is funded by major gum manufacturers including Mars Wrigley and Perfetti Van Melle, with an investment of up to £10 million spread over 5 years In the past 4 years, the Task Force has awarded grants worth a total of £6.46 million, funded the cleaning of over 4.15 million square metres of pavements

title: Rochdale man convicted following fraudulent COVID-19 claim - Rochdale Borough Council

input.evidence_text: A Rochdale man who claimed more than £22,000 in COVID-19 business grants he wasn’t entitled to has been handed a suspended sentence and ordered to pay all the money back Mohammad Kabil Saddique, 33, of Clayton Street, Rochdale, claimed to be running a car business, known as Medallion Motors Limited, from Fieldhouse Industrial Estate in Rochdale and made 6 successful separate claims for covid assistance between May 2020 and April 2021. In total, h Investigations by our counter fraud team and Greater Manchester Police, which were supported by intelligence from the National Fraud Initiative, revealed that he had deliberately falsified his business lease documents and that he had only ever used a pigeonhole at the industrial Mr Saddique admitted 2 counts of fraud by false representation at Manchester’s Minshull Street Crown Court on Friday, 5 June 2026 In addition, he also pleaded guilty to theft of a motor vehicle at the same hearing, after he ordered a car costing more than £58,000 from an Audi dealership and cancelled the payment while the car was already on its way to him. Police eventually tracked down the stolen Audi RS3, He was sentenced to 22 months imprisonment, suspended for 18 months, 270 hours of unpaid work and 15 rehabilitation activity days. He has also been ordered to pay all the money back

title: Great Lever is cleaner and greener thanks to council's action against fly-tipping - Bolton Council

input.lead: Residents in Great Lever are seeing a cleaner, greener neighbourhood, with fly-tipping on the decline thanks to a proactive environmental enforcement project
input.summary: Residents in Great Lever are seeing a cleaner, greener neighbourhood, with fly-tipping on the decline thanks to a proactive environmental enforcement project
input.evidence_text: Residents in Great Lever are seeing a cleaner, greener neighbourhood, with fly-tipping on the decline thanks to a proactive environmental enforcement project Work led by the council’s environmental enforcement team has delivered clear improvements across the ward, with targeted action at known hotspots helping to reduce incidents and improve the appearance of local streets and open spaces Between April and June this year, investigations into fly-tipping offences have resulted in Fixed Penalty Notices and warning letters being issued, helping to deter offenders and reinforce the message that environmental crime will not be tolerated A combination of enforcement, partnership working and community engagement has been central to the project’s success. Officers have worked closely with housing standards, neighbourhood services and residents to ensure issues are dealt with quickly and do not escalate Problem areas across the ward have seen significant improvements. Untidy land has been cleared and brought back into use, including sites at Asia Mill, Forrester Hill and along Manchester Road, where landowners have taken action following interventions New measures have also helped tackle persistent fly-tipping hotspots. CCTV has been introduced in key locations, leading to fines being issued, while regular patrols and rapid response clean-ups have ensured waste is removed quickly before further dumping can occur

title: Encouraging decline in youth smoking and vaping across Wigan Borough

input.lead: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.summary: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.evidence_text: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally and protect the health of our children and young people With more than 1,000 school children aged 14-17 included in the Trading Standards survey, the findings show the number of young people who have tried vaping dropped from around 44 per cent in 2023 to 18 per cent in 2026 The survey, carried out between November 2025 and February 2026, also highlights that smoking is at an all-time low, with around 97 per cent reporting to have never tried smoking Councillor Keith Cunliffe, Deputy Leader of Wigan Council and cabinet portfolio holder for adult social care and health, said: “I’m really pleased to see smoking and vaping rates among young people in Wigan Borough continuing to fall “Reducing tobacco and nicotine use among young people is one of the most effective ways to prevent long-term ill health and reduce health inequalities in our communities

input.evidence_text: The Mayor unveils the new Blue Plaque for Dr John Garton on Park Road, Golborne A renowned Wigan Borough scientist has received a heritage Blue Plaque outside his former home in Golborne Dr John Garton’s (1863–1922) pioneering work in crop manufacturing has had a lasting impact across the globe The Blue Plaque was unveiled at a ceremony on Park Road, Golborne, attended by the Mayor of Wigan Borough Councillor Chris Ready, cabinet portfolio holder for communities and neighbourhoods, said: “Dr John Garton’s work has helped transform farming practices and improve sustainability, making him a significant figure not just locally, but across the world “The Blue Plaque scheme is designed to celebrate the impact residents of our borough have made and Dr Garton’s legacy and influence in the world of agriculture will live on.”

title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

input.evidence_text: Sounds of the City: Wet Leg - Manchester’s Finest Sounds of the City: Wet Leg Wed 8th Jul, 2026 - Castlefield Bowl Sounds of the City: Wet Leg Castlefield Bowl, Rice Street, Manchester M3 4JR Wed 8th Jul, 2026 5.30pm General admission £44.60 Book tickets Directions events Go to Manchester's Finest Facebook page Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Few bands have risen quite as quickly as Wet Leg. The Isle of Wight outfit exploded onto the scene with Chaise Longue and have spent the years since collecting awards, selling out tours and building a reputation as one of Britain’s most entertaining live acts. Their Sounds of the City show – part of the ever-brilliant summer series of gigs at Castlefield Bowl – promises plenty of the sharp post-punk riffs, huge choruses and a tongue-in-cheek approach that’s made them huge indie stars. input.evidence_text: An Evening With Simon Shaw & Alfredo Arribas - Manchester’s Finest An Evening With Simon Shaw & Alfredo Arribas Wed 8th Jul, 2026 - El Gato Negro An Evening With Simon Shaw & Alfredo Arribas El Gato Negro, 52 King St, Manchester M2 4LY Wed 8th Jul, 2026 6.30pm - 9.30pm £98 Get tickets Directions events Go to Manchester's Finest Facebook page Go to Manchester's Finest X profile El Gato chef and founder Simon Shaw is creating a one-night-only multi-course menu built around wines from acclaimed Priorat producer Alfredo Arribas, from one of Spain’s most respected wine regions. Known for its steep Catalan vineyards, slate soils and bold, expressive reds, Priorat has become a benchmark for wines with proper depth and real character. Each course has been designed to complement a different bottle, with guests guided through the pairings across the evening, alongside tasting notes, producer insights, bespoke dishes from the kitchen, and the chance to purchase bottles to take home afterwards. После аншлагов в Европе и США спектакль впервые приезжает в Лондон. 📍 University College London, Level 1, 20 Bedford Way, London WC1H 0AL 🕕 DOORS: 18:00 🕖 SHOW: 19:00 «Скамейка» — знаменитая пьеса, когда-то блистательно сыгранная Олегом Ефремовым и Татьяной Дорониной на сцене МХАТа, а затем покорившая театральные площадки по всему миру. Это история о мужчине и женщине. О случайной встрече, которая постепенно превращается в откровенный разговор — честный, смешной, ранимый и очень человеческий. О том моменте, когда между людьми исчезают роли и, наконец, становится невозможно притворяться. На сцене — Алексей Панин и Ольга Силина. Тонкая актерская игра, живая энергия и удивительно узнаваемая история, в которой каждый услышит что-то своё. “Скамейка” — спектакль, после которого хочется немного помолчать и еще долго думать о главном. Если вам не хватает настоящего театра — этот вечер нельзя пропустить. Количество билетов ограничено. The move marks a new chapter for the pair, who will also launch Boutikk, a neighbouring provisions store inspired by Scandinavian food culture and tr Originally opened in 2020, OSMA quickly established itself as one of Greater Manchester’s most acclaimed neighbourhood restaurants. Named after a combination of Oslo and Manchester, the restaurant blended Stoermann-Naess’ Norwegian heritage with Heron’s classical culinary trainin The return will see Heron resume her role as Executive Chef, with a menu centred around seasonal produce, fresh seafood, local meats and vegetable-led dishes. A number of OSMA’s most popular plates are expected to make a comeback, alongside the restaurant’s well-known sharing Sun While the restaurant occupies one side of the development, Boutikk will introduce a new offering to the city centre. The hard part is getting at them. If you run your business across more than one system, you'll know the feeling. Your numbers are in your accounts, your customers are in your CRM, your sales and stock are somewhere else again. Getting a straight answer usually means exporting from each system and stitching it together by hand. There's a better way, and it goes well beyond asking a quick question. In this session, Craig Morrall, co-founder of Tugger, will show you how to get Claude doing real work with your business data. Not one-line answers, but full reports, dashboards, and reporting that runs itself. Four things most people don't know Claude can do: Pull two systems together in one question. Ask something that spans your accounts and your CRM at once and get one clear answer, the thing no single app can do. Build a full board pack in minutes. Watch Claude pull the numbers, write the commentary, and hand you a finished document you'd take to the board. Spin up a dashboard you can interact with. An interactive dashboard built from a single prompt. No Power BI skills, no formulas, no faff. Put your reporting on autopilot. Set it up once so the routine stuff, like the Monday morning report, gets done before anyone sits down. This is made for any business running on more than one system, whatever your sector. Already a Tugger customer? You'll pick up plenty to get more from what you've already got. New to us? You'll see exactly what's possible with your own data. Book N

title: musicMagpie's Steve Oliver follows Pep in announcing exit

input.lead: Steve Oliver has announced his exit from musicMagpie.
input.summary: Steve Oliver has announced his exit from musicMagpie. Oliver famously co-founded the business with Walter Gleeson from his Stockport garage in 2007 and will leave his role as managing director in September. After first revealing his intention at the company’s annual conference, h
input.evidence_text: musicMagpie’s Steve Oliver follows Pep in announcing exit Posted on July 3, 2026 by Chris Maguire musicMagpie’s Steve Oliver follows Pep in announcing exit musicMagpie founder and lifelong Man City fan Steve Oliver has announced he’ll be leaving the company and handing the reins to Jonathan Miller Share: Founders Steve Oliver has announced his exit from musicMagpie. Oliver famously co-founded the business with Walter Gleeson from his Stockport garage in 2007 and will leave his role as managing director in September. After first revealing his intention at the company’s annual conference, he took to LinkedIn to reveal his near-20-year journey with the company will come to an end on September 30. Originally set up as the ‘lazy man’s eBay’, musicMagpie allowed people to trade in their CDs, DVDs and games for cash. With developments in technology, it pivoted towards phones and gadgets and floated on the London Stock Exchange’s junior AIM market in April 2021 with a share price of 193p. AO buys musicMagpie: ‘Life was pretty tough as a standalone PLC’ That represented a market capitalisation of £208m. However, musicMagpie’s share price dropped to almost 5p in 2024 before fellow London-listed company AO stepped in with an offer of 9.07p in cash per share, valuing the business at just under £10m . Just like his hero, former Man City manager Pep Guardiola, he said it felt like the right time to leave. Oliver said: “My greatest single pride at Magpie has always been the people …. more so than our cumulative £2bn of gross income in our history so far, more so than the little business that started in my garage in Hazel Grove becoming the biggest seller in the world in the history of eBay and Amazon and more so than paying out well over £1/2bn pounds in tax-free cash to our customers

title: V&A to bring David Bowie Archive to Blackpool's Showtown

input.lead: The V&A has announced a national tour of highlights from its David Bowie archive, including a stint at Blackpool’s award-winning Showtown museum David Bowie: On Tour will run in Blackpool from Wed 30 jun 2027 – Sun 26 Sep.
input.summary: The V&A has announced a national tour of highlights from its David Bowie archive, including a stint at Blackpool’s award-winning Showtown museum David Bowie: On Tour will run in Blackpool from Wed 30 jun 2027 – Sun 26 Sep. The experience features more than 100 highlights from the

Eat, meet, drink, shop, watch, talk, listen, laugh. Come in, be yourself and if you like it bring your friends! Altrincham’s charter dates back to 1290, it’s location at the intersection of the Cheshire farm belt and the commercial commuter zone of Manchester means the town has a long and important history. Altrincham market is now at the heart of the regeneration of ‘the modern market tow The covered market hosts regional food producers, vintage fashion and furniture, home & garden and contemporary craft on a Tuesday, Friday, Saturday and Sunday Altrincham Market House Opening Hours Tues-Sat : 9am-10pm Sun : 9am-6pm Mon : Closed The Outdoor Market (Shopping) Tues: 8am-3pm Fri: 8am-3pm Sat: 8am-4pm Sun: 10am-4pm Altrincham Market House is home to award-winning independent quality food operators. title: Bolton Car Boot Sale

input.lead: Bolton Car Boot Sale at Bolton Car Boot Sale
input.summary: Huge Sunday car boot at Bolton's Macron Stadium (BL6 6JW), Feb-Dec. 300+ stalls, £1 per car entry, catering, toilets and dog-friendly Event date: 2026-07-12 Venue: Bolton Car Boot Sale Price: £1 Booking: https://carbootdirectory.co.uk/listings/bolton-car-boot/ Status: https://sch
input.evidence_text: Bolton Car Boot Sale | Bolton Sunday Car Boot Sale | Times, Prices & Info Skip to content 210+ UK car boot sales — find one near you this weekend Type your postcode or town to find sales near you Search car boot sales About This Car Boot Sale Bolton Car Boot Sale is one of the North West's biggest weekly boot sales, held in the hardstanding car park of the Macron Stadium (formerly Reebok), Burnden Way, Horwich, Bolton BL6 6JW. It runs every Sunday from February right through to December, drawing over 300 sellers and thousands of bargain hunters each week. Sellers can arrive and set up from 6:00 AM, with buyers welcomed in from 7:00 AM. Entry costs just £1 per car for buyers. Pitches for sellers are £14 for a car, £20 for a van and £3 for a trailer, with regular and corner pitches available on request. Bring exact change to keep the queue moving, and call organiser Andrew on 07470784644 for more details. Expect hundreds of stalls across the concrete hardstanding pitches, so it goes ahead in most weather. On-site you'll find a 5-star catering service, doughnuts and coffee, ice cream and desserts, a meat auction, fresh fruit and veg, plants, and retro sweet stalls. The sale is dog-friendly, has male and female toilets, ample parking, and kids' rides and bouncy castles to keep little ones entertained. Last confirmed open: 8 June 2026. title: It Walks Around The House At Night

input.lead: It Walks Around The House At Night
input.summary: A ghost story by Tim Foley · Directed by Neil Bettles Following a sell-out UK tour and London run, the award-winning ThickSkin present this chilling new play by Tim Foley (former Bruntwood Prize-winner) When an out-of-work actor takes a job playing a ghost at an old countryside m
input.evidence_text: A ghost story by Tim Foley · Directed by Neil Bettles Following a sell-out UK tour and London run, the award-winning ThickSkin present this chilling new play by Tim Foley (former Bruntwood Prize-winner) When an out-of-work actor takes a job playing a ghost at an old countryside manor, he expects nothing more than a few cheap scares. But as night after night unfolds, the role begins to blur with reality — and something far more sinister starts to emerge Part ghost story, part psychological thriller, It Walks Around The House At Night draws audiences into a world of creeping dread, unexpected twists and dark humour. A haunted house story for a new era, brought to life through inventive staging, immersive sound and bold visual eff ThickSkin, one of the UK’s most exciting touring companies, joins forces with Tim Foley (award-winning writer of Doctor Who audio dramas; Electric Rosary, Royal Exchange Theatre) to create this thrilling production. Directed by Neil Bettles (ThickSkin’s How Not To Drown, Disney’s Unsettling, darkly comic and full of surprises, the stage is set… for terror! Age Recommendation: 14+ Content Warnings: Loud and sudden noises, haze, strobe, flashing lights, occasional strong language, horror imagery and jump scares. Runtime: 90 minutes (no interval)

title: Who is Jeremy Monga? 16-year-old at centre of huge Man City transfer

input.lead: Manchester City are close to completing a £10million transfer for Leicester City teenager Jeremy Monga
input.summary: Manchester City are close to completing a £10million transfer for Leicester City teenager Jeremy Monga
input.evidence_text: Football . Manchester City Transfer News . . Who is Jeremy Monga? 16-year-old at centre of huge Man City transfer. Manchester City are close to completing a £10million transfer for Leicester City teenager Jeremy Monga. . . View Image . . . Jeremy Monga. . . . . . . . Comments . . Sport . Ross Jackson Head of Football . . 14:00, 04 Jul 2026 . . . Here's all you need to know about Jeremy Monga and Manchester City's move for the youngster .... City monitored Monga last summer but ultimately decided against making a move.. Monga then signed a one-year scholarship deal with Leicester that would become a professional deal when he turned 17.. Arsenal were expected to complete a move for Monga this summer but the arrival of Enzo Maresca saw City reignite their interest in the youngster.. Maresca was Foxes boss while Monga was coming through the ranks in the East Midlands and so he knows the player and his talent better than most.. Monga has already made 34 senior appearances for Leicester, despite not turning 17 until July 10, and even made his Premier League debut at the age of 15 when he came on as a substitute against Newcastle United in April 2025.. Only Arsenal pair Max Dowman and Ethan Nwaneri have appeared in a top-flight fixture at a younger age.. Monga has played most of his football at Leicester on the left but can also play centrally or on the right.. Monga is expected to be handed a chance to shine on City's pre-season tour of Asia this summer, with a whole host of Blues stars still away at the World Cup

input.evidence_text: 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне Андрей Кравченко покорил сердца тысяч слушателей благодаря своему уникальному голосу, харизме и способности превращать личные истории в песни, близкие каждому. В его творчестве смешиваются романтическая лирика, современное эстрадное звучание и проникновенная искренность После серии аншлаговых выступлений на самых престижных сценах Украины, среди которых Жовтневый Дворец и Дворец “Украина”, певец продолжает свое концертное путешествие и приглашает поклонников на особую встречу в Лондоне Концерты Андрея Кравченко известны своей теплой атмосферой: здесь нет дистанции между сценой и залом, ведь каждое выступление превращается в откровенный разговор на языке музыки. Гостей ждут любимые хиты, живой звук, море эмоций и моменты, которые захочется сохранить в памяти над Важной частью творческой миссии артиста остается поддержка Украины — часть средств от концерта будет передана на помощь ВСУ! Подарите себе вечер настоящей романтики, красивой музыки и искренних чувств вместе с Андреем Кравченко

input.summary: Дронотур Бумбокса Європою. Листопад-грудень 2026 року
input.evidence_text: Дронотур Бумбокса Європою. Листопад-грудень 2026 року. Презентація максі-синглу "Живий" Кожного разу, коли перед менеджментом гурту Бумбокс стоїть завдання написати пресреліз, ми починаємо згадувати цікаві факти. Скільки концертів зіграли, скільки грошей зібрали, які пісні видали. Це база. Непогано ще пам’ятати, чи був гурт у країні, до якої ми збираємося, чи це пер О, як би хотілося написати: "Концерт гурту Бумбокс" — і як у Львові за добу продано 8000 квитків. Так-так, ми не про Львів розповідаємо, хоча теж про Європу Наші віддані слухачі, якщо ви перебуваєте за кордоном, сподіваємось, вам буде цікаво дізнатися, що після традиційної перерви на декілька місяців, Бумбокс поїде в турне від Ізраїлю до Німеччини, від Англії до Польщі, від Ірландії до Словаччини та Чехії І повеземо ми в це турне свіженький, як херсонський кавун, максі-сингл “Живий”, підсилений піснями з різних альбомів, під частину яких виросли ви, а під частину зростають ваші діти Тож ознайомтеся, будь ласка, уважно з розкладом туру, купуйте квитки заздалегідь, готуйте гроші на донати та мерч — зустрінемося в листопаді

Cheltenham Borough Council and Gloucester City Council confirmed on Tuesday (June 30) the Staverton site would brought back to market, with property firm Savills appointed to lead a renewed The news comes just three months after the sale of the loss-making transport hub fell through after months of negotiations. input.evidence_text: Economic Development Politics 'No 10 North' will help power flow into West Country, says Labour leader hopeful Andy Burnham The MP for Makerfield has made his first major policy speech since launching a bid to become PM Comments Economic Development Hannah Baker South West Busine Labour leader hopeful Andy Burnham has pledged to create a 'No 10 North' if he becomes Prime Minister, claiming it will help power flow into regions including the West Country The newly elected MP for Makerfield, who is currently the party's frontrunner to take over from Sir Keir Starmer, unveiled plans on Monday (June 29) to devolve more powers to the UK's regions and nations In his first major policy speech since launching his leadership bid, the former Greater Manchester mayor said he would deliver the "biggest change in our lifetime to the way the country is run" while remaining "consistent" to Labour's 2024 manifesto "We will create a more streamlined state with a clearer purpose to power up all parts of the country and put a laser-like focus on growth and regeneration," he said "The change will be driven through the prime minister’s office in an extended operation based here in Manchester.
